task: We’ve got a coastal storm coming and I’m trying to get my place squared away before anything goes sideways. Can you help me make a simple, no-nonsense order for what to secure first, what to keep within reach, and what to do if the building loses power or starts taking on water?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a simple order that fits a coastal storm setup:

1. Secure first
- Bring in or tie down anything loose outside: chairs, bins, tools, planters, grill.
- Close and latch windows, storm doors, and anything that can blow open.
- Move valuables, electronics, and important papers up high.
- If you have a basement, get anything irreplaceable out of it.

2. Keep within reach
- Flashlights, batteries, phone chargers, power bank
- Meds, glasses, keys, wallet, ID
- Water, snacks, pet supplies
- Radio, weather app backup, list of emergency numbers
- Towels, buckets, trash bags, duct tape

3. If the building loses power
- Turn off and unplug sensitive electronics.
- Check fridge/freezer and keep the doors closed.
- Use flashlights, not candles if you can avoid it.
- If you use a generator, keep it outside only, far from windows and doors.
- Check on neighbors if it’s safe.

4. If water starts coming in
- Move stuff up and away from the floor right away.
- Put towels or barriers at the entry point if that helps slow it.
- Don’t walk through standing water if you can avoid it.
- If water is near outlets, the breaker box, or major appliances, shut power off only if you can do it safely and dry.
- Take photos and short notes once it’s safe: where water came in, how high it got, what got wet.

5. After things settle
- Dry and separate damaged items.
- Save receipts for anything you buy to clean up or replace.
- Contact landlord, building management, or insurer if needed.
- Keep notes on time, water level, power loss, and any repairs.

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page storm checklist you can use tonight.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly fine. I’d tighten it up more, because in a real storm you don’t want a list that wanders.

Put it like this:

1. First
- papers, cash, meds, IDs
- phone, charger, battery radio, flashlight, batteries
- move anything valuable or ruined by water up high
- bring in loose junk outside

2. Keep handy
- water
- snacks
- keys, wallet
- glasses
- towels, garbage bags, duct tape
- boots or shoes you can use in wet mess

3. If the power goes
- fridge closed
- unplug what matters
- no candles if you can help it
- use the radio, keep the phone charged
- if you’ve got a generator, it stays outside. people still die doing that stupid thing

4. If water comes in
- get stuff off the floor fast
- stay out of standing water if there’s any chance of live wires
- kill power only if you can do it dry and safe
- block the entry if you can
- take pictures once it’s safe

5. After
- write down what got hit and when
- keep receipts
- call landlord or insurer

That’s the order. I’d also add one thing: if you’re in a basement or low unit, pack the bag now, not later. I learned that the hard way back when the basement in my building took water. Once it starts, everybody gets busy pretending they’ll come back tomorrow.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that’s the right order. Tight version:

1. First
- papers, cash, meds, IDs
- phone, charger, battery radio, flashlight, batteries
- move valuables and anything water-sensitive up high
- bring in loose stuff outside

2. Keep handy
- water
- snacks
- keys, wallet
- glasses
- towels, trash bags, duct tape
- boots or shoes for wet mess

3. If the power goes
- keep fridge/freezer closed
- unplug sensitive electronics
- no candles if you can avoid it
- use the radio, keep the phone charged
- generator stays outside, away from doors and windows

4. If water comes in
- get stuff off the floor fast
- stay out of standing water if there’s any chance of live wires
- only shut off power if you can do it dry and safely
- block the entry if you can
- take pictures once it’s safe

5. After
- write down what got hit and when
- keep receipts
- call landlord or insurer

And yes: if you’re in a basement or low unit, pack the bag now, not later.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the order. Clean enough, no fluff.

And the basement line matters. People always act like they’ve got time right up until they don’t. I’ve seen that game already.
